claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your circumstances.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you some questions. Remember that Child Benefit is assessed for each child individually.

First, could you please tell me: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes I'm looking to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my daughter Alex who I'm responsible for.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I understand you have one daughter, Alex, and you're responsible for her.

To assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask: **How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Since Alex is 8 years old (under 16), that's good for eligibility purposes.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Now I need to ask: **Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week, or is she not in paid work at all?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she doesn't work at all - she's only 8, so she's still in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that makes sense. Next question: **Is Alex receiving any benefits in her own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she doesn't receive any benefits in her own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Good. Now, **is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's not in an apprenticeship - she's only 8, so she's just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Now I need to ask about your circumstances. 

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now, regarding your responsibility for Alex:

**Do you live with Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Good. Now I need to ask: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or might anyone else claim for her?** (For example, Alex's other parent or another family member?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to check Alex's living situation:

**Is Alex currently in any of the following situations:**
- In hospital?
- In local authority care?
- Being fostered?
- Or is she living normally at home with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I'm fostering Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that you're fostering Alex. This is an important detail for Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I need to ask: **Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance as part of the fostering arrangement?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't get anything from the council for fostering her.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! Based on all the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16), so she qualifies as a child for Child Benefit purposes
- She's not working or receiving benefits in her own right
- You normally live in the UK (in Manchester)
- You live with Alex and are responsible for her
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her
- The local council is not paying towards her accommodation or maintenance

**Next steps to claim:**
- You can apply online or by post using form CH2
- You'll need to provide proof of identity for yourself and Alex
- You'll need Alex's birth certificate or adoption certificate
- Be aware that if you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, the High Income Child Benefit Charge may apply
- Payments are typically made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

Do you have any questions about claiming Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's great news, thank you so much for explaining everything so clearly! I understand what I need to do now. I'll get started on the application.
